one_line_ask: Run the client's authored InDesign rate-card template through an InDesign data-merge against a supplied auto-insurance premium-tier CSV, place the on-brand logo and licensed background texture, refresh the compliance disclosure footer, and export a press-ready PDF plus a high-resolution JPEG.
brief: Beacon Mutual Insurance Group, a regional personal-lines insurance broker, needs its quarterly Personal Auto rate card finished for print and for the branch counters. Our design lead has already built and handed off the authored InDesign template (auto_rate_card.indd) — it is fully brand-locked to our style guide (Beacon Slate #1F2A44 and Signal Amber #F4A82C, headline face Basis Grotesque, body face Source Serif) and carries a single-page A4 portrait rate table with REAL InDesign data-merge fields (<<coverage_tier>>, <<coverage_desc>>, <<annual_premium>>, <<monthly_premium>>, <<deductible>>) laid out across four coverage bands (Liability Only, Standard, Standard Plus, Full Coverage), a logo placement frame, a full-bleed background frame, and a boxed disclosure/footer frame. We are NOT asking you to redesign the layout — the layout is locked. What we need is the production pass: (1) bind our supplied Q3 premium data (auto_premium_tiers.csv) into the template via a proper InDesign data merge so every tier, description, annual/monthly premium and deductible lands in the right cell — accuracy is critical, this is regulated pricing and one wrong number is a reprint; (2) place our brand
